details: Package 'redis-type-os' ships what appears to be a verbatim copy of the legitimate 'redis-om' / 'redis-om-node' source (same exports, same Client/Repository/Schema classes, same author attribution 'Guy Royse'), and its package.json points repository at github:redis/redis-om-node despite being published under a different, unrelated name. No malicious behavior is present in the shipped code: there are no lifecycle scripts, no outbound network I/O beyond the normal redis client, no obfuscation, and no credential reads. The package does declare a 'void-ulid' dependency that is not part of the upstream redis-om dependency set, which warrants human review of that transitive. Name-similarity and source-repackaging without a payload are subjective signals and require adjudicator review rather than an automatic block.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
